summary: 2026-06-24 19:45:26 PDT — Reviewed `git diff main...HEAD`, `git log --oneline main..HEAD`, and `reviews/graceful-shutdown.md`. The shutdown implementation itself is scoped and largely matches the spec; I found one acceptance-test gap that can false-pass without proving the signal path ran. `npm test` could not be validated in this sandbox because listening/temp-file operations are denied with `EPERM`.

findings.title: Shutdown test can pass without sending a signal
findings.claim: `bootThenSignal` resolves with the child exit code regardless of whether the `server on` log was observed or `child.kill(signal)` was ever called. A child that exits 0 before listening would make both SIGTERM/SIGINT tests pass without verifying the required sequence: boot, wait for listen, send signal, then assert clean exit 0.
findings.suggestion: Track whether the listen log was seen and reject in the `exit`/`close` handler if `signalled` is false; also check the return value of `child.kill(signal)` and reject if the signal was not delivered.
